title: Sideritis × vallisoletana
taxonomy: Sideritis × vallisoletana is classified as a hybrid plant, specifically a cross between two species of the genus Sideritis. This hybrid plant is part of the Lamiaceae family, which includes mint and basil. It is native to the Iberian Peninsula and is also known as the "Spanish ironwort." The classification of Sideritis × vallisoletana is further specified as a nothospecies, indicating that it is a stable and fertile hybrid. As a hybrid, Sideritis × vallisoletana exhibits characteristics of both its parent species.
uses: Sideritis × vallisoletana, a plant native to the Iberian Peninsula, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits. Its leaves and flowers are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. In traditional medicine, it is used to treat various ailments, including fever, bronchitis, and digestive issues. Additionally, it is sometimes used as a natural remedy for stress and anxiety due to its calming effects. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential interactions with other medications.
benefits: Sideritis × vallisoletana, also known as the Spanish giant or giant scabious, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits. This plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are believed to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Studies suggest that Sideritis × vallisoletana may have a positive effect on cardiovascular health by reducing inflammation and improving lipid profiles. Additionally, its extracts have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal activities, making it a potential natural remedy for infections. Further research is needed to fully understand the extent of its health benefits and potential applications.
properties: Sideritis × vallisoletana, also known as Vallisoletan Ironwort, is a plant species that has been traditionally used for its therapeutic properties. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. Its extracts have been studied for their potential to have antimicrobial and antiviral properties, which could help to combat infections. In traditional medicine, Sideritis × vallisoletana is used to treat various conditions, including respiratory issues and gastrointestinal problems. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of the plant's therapeutic properties.
constituents: Sideritis × vallisoletana is known to contain a range of bioactive compounds. The main active constituents of Sideritis × vallisoletana include flavonoids and phenolic acids, particularly 6-hydroxykaempferol and 6-hydroxykaempferol 3-O-β-D-glucoside. These compounds have been reported to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. In addition, the plant also contains other secondary metabolites, such as terpenoids and alkaloids, which contribute to its bioactivity. The combination of these active constituents may contribute to the plant's traditional uses in herbal medicine.

intro: Sideritis × vallisoletana, commonly known as Spanish giant ironwort, is a perennial plant species within the Sideritis genus, native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which contribute to its potential in treating various health issues, such as digestive problems, respiratory issues, and wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Sideritis × vallisoletana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Sideritis × vallisoletana is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and capsules, as well as being incorporated into various pharmaceutical formulations.
